reasoning: The national 12-month CD rate (NDR12MCD) has shown a clear downward trend in the second half of 2025, falling from 1.76% in August to 1.64% in November. This decline tracks the broader federal funds rate, which the Fed has been gradually cutting. Strong market consensus (as of late October 2025) anticipated an additional 25-basis-point rate cut at the October 28-29 FOMC meeting, with further cuts expected in December 2025 and into 2026. Because CD rates typically lead or move in tandem with Fed rate cuts, we can expect the national 12-month rate to continue falling in the near term. Therefore, the probability that the CD rate will have *increased* by December 2025 or February 2026 relative to the November 9, 2025 baseline (which is currently 1.64%) is relatively low. I assign an 18% probability for the December 2025 horizon, reflecting the near certainty of the ongoing downward adjustments from recent policy cuts. For the February 2026 horizon, I widen the probability to 28% to account for increased uncertainty and the potential that banks might momentarily pause their deposit rate reductions.
